info.title: Slothbox API
info.description:
REST API behind Slothbox. Use it to manage organizations, members, invites and AWS connections. Most endpoints accept either a Cognito ID token or an API key (`sk_…`) in the `Authorization` header.

**Rate limiting.** Requests are limited per caller — per API key, per user, and per organization on expensive routes. When a limit is exceeded the API responds `429 Too Many Requests` with a `Retry-After` header (whole seconds); wait that long before retrying. Expensive routes (AWS connection checks, environment launches, billing, invite emails, GitHub calls) carry tighter limits than the default. Full table: https://docs.slothbox.dev/rate-limiting (source: docs/rate-limiting.md).

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description:
Owner-managed outbound webhooks. Slothbox POSTs a signed event to your
HTTPS endpoint the moment something happens in the org.

**Verifying deliveries.** Each POST carries three headers — `webhook-id`
(stable across retries; use it as your idempotency key), `webhook-timestamp`
(unix seconds), and `webhook-signature` (`v1,<base64 HMAC-SHA256>`; a
space-delimited list during a secret rotation — a match against any token
is valid). The signature is computed over `{webhook-id}.{webhook-timestamp}.{raw body}`
using your `whsec_…` secret fed verbatim (the verifier base64-decodes it after
stripping the prefix). Verify the RAW body bytes (not re-serialized JSON), use a
constant-time compare, and reject timestamps more than 5 minutes (300s) old.

Verify with the `svix` libraries (`new Webhook(secret).verify(rawBody, headers)`)
or a few lines of HMAC. Full guide + copy-paste Node receivers:
https://docs.slothbox.dev/webhooks (source: docs/webhooks-consumers.md).

apiKey.name: Authorization
apiKey.description: Send an API key (`sk_…`) as the raw value of the `Authorization` header. An optional `Bearer ` prefix is accepted — the authorizer strips it (case-insensitively) before validating, so `Authorization: sk_…` and `Authorization: Bearer sk_…` both work. Two kinds exist: per-seat **personal** keys for light scripting, and org **service-account** keys for headless/unattended automation (gated behind the API plan). Cognito ID tokens use the same header, but those are only useful for the web app.

Unauthorized.description: Missing or invalid credentials. Send an API key (`sk_…`) or Cognito ID token in the `Authorization` header (an optional `Bearer ` prefix is accepted).

RateLimited.description: Rate limit exceeded. Limits apply per caller — per API key, per user, and per organization on expensive routes. Wait the number of seconds given in the `Retry-After` header before retrying.

Retry-After.description: How long to wait before retrying, in whole seconds.

authMethod.description: How the caller authenticated. `apiKey` covers both personal and service-account keys.

mfaEnabled.description: Whether the user has a TOTP authenticator enrolled.

hasSeenGuide.description: Whether the user has seen the first-visit web-app guide.

permissions.description: The caller's granted capabilities in this org (empty for owners).

Error.description: Standard error envelope returned for any non-2xx response.

requireMfa.description: When true, members must enrol MFA to use the org in the web app.
Owner only. Enforce MFA for all members.

permissions.description: Granted capabilities for a member. Owners hold every permission implicitly.

mfaEnabled.description: Whether the member has a TOTP authenticator enrolled.

permissions.description: The complete set of capabilities to grant the member (replaces existing).

userId.description: The member who should become the new owner.

days.description: Days the window starts on; 0 = Sunday.

PartialAutoSleepPolicy.description: Per-template auto-sleep override (SLO-22); overlays the org policy per-leaf.

needsRebake.description: True when a ready template was baked on an older bake recipe than current — its AMI is stale and should be re-baked.

draftState.description: Present only while status is `draft` — the saved builder state to resume from. Cleared on publish.

draft.description: Marks this as a draft save: the contents are stored as-is and not validated until the template is published.

name.description: Optional for drafts — shown as "Untitled draft" when absent.

draftState.description: Opaque blob of the builder's in-progress state, rehydrated by the web UI when the draft is resumed.

serviceKeys.description: Catalog keys of the template's services, one per service (may repeat). Drives the service icons shown on the templates list.

projectCount.description: Number of projects in the template.

EnvironmentMetricsSample.description: Latest host-metrics snapshot reported on a `health` heartbeat.

mine.description: True when the authenticated caller launched this box. Lifecycle ops (start/stop/terminate/connect) are restricted to the launcher or a member with the environments:delete permission (which owners hold implicitly).

lastStartedAt.description: When the box was last (re)started after a stop.

autoStopReason.description: Set when the box was auto-stopped by the auto-sleep policy (SLO-22). Cleared on a manual start.

projectSetup.description: Per-project clone/setup status reported by the serve agent (SLO-38).

samples.description: Server-side downsampled to at most 240 time-bucketed average points.

allOf.description: The most recent snapshot from the env row, or null.

quickUpdateUrl.description: One-click CloudFormation console link to update the stack with the new template. Present when the stack ARN was captured (callback v7+).

connectionId.description: AWS connection whose account stores this org’s secrets.

value.description: Present only for variables (fetched from the org account). Secret values are never returned.

repo.description: repo scope: normalized git URL, for display.

billingRequired.description: True when the trial has ended and no card is on file.

includedSeats.description: Seats included free by the API plan (0 when the plan is inactive).

billableSeats.description: Seats actually billed = max(0, seatCount − includedSeats).

totalPence.description: Billable seats × unit price, plus the API plan price when active.

unitPricePence.description: API plan price for the current billing interval (monthly or annual).

apiPlan.description: Standalone API plan; its flat price is included in totalPence when active.

eventTypes.description: Subscribed event types, or ['*'] for every type.

ordered.description: When true, deliveries are strictly ordered per endpoint (FIFO).

webhookId.description: Stable id, sent as the `webhook-id` header and reused verbatim across retries — use it as your idempotency key.

get.description: Catalog of services environment templates can include (postgres, redis, dynamodb-local, mysql). Populates the "Add service" picker in the web UI.

200.description: Array of catalog services with available versions

get.description: Catalog of language runtimes that projects can declare via the `runtime:` field (e.g. `node:22`). Populates the runtime dropdown in the project form.

200.description: Array of catalog runtimes with available versions
Caller identity plus the orgs they belong to

mfaRequired.description: True when any of the caller’s orgs enforce MFA.

post.summary: Generate a per-org Git SSH key for the caller
post.description: Generates an ed25519 keypair server-side, stores the private key KMS-encrypted, and pushes the public key to the GitHub account bound for this user in this org. Used by Slothbox dev environments launched inside the org to clone, pull, and push to private repos. Replaces any existing key for this (org, user) -- the previous GitHub-side key is revoked first.

201.description: New key generated and pushed to GitHub

403.description: Only the user can manage their own SSH key

412.description: GitHub not linked for this org, or linked without the write:public_key scope

get.summary: Get the caller's per-org Git SSH key metadata
get.description: Returns the public key + fingerprint for the caller in this org. The private key is never returned via the API; it lives KMS-encrypted in DynamoDB and is only emitted into env bootstrap user-data at launch time.

403.description: Only the user can read their own SSH key

404.description: No Git SSH key generated yet

delete.summary: Revoke the caller's per-org Git SSH key
delete.description: Removes the key from GitHub (best-effort -- using the org's bound GitHub token, if still available) and forgets the encrypted private key.

403.description: Only the user can manage their own SSH key

get.summary: Get the caller’s GitHub link, if any

200.description: Either an unlinked sentinel or the linked account summary.

get.summary: List the caller’s linked external accounts
get.description: The caller’s connection library across providers. Optionally filter with `?provider=github`. Tokens are never returned.

schema.description: Filter to one provider. Unknown values are ignored (no filter).

200.description: Linked accounts owned by the caller.

post.description: The caller automatically becomes the first owner.

200.description: Orgs the caller is a member of.

awsUpdateAvailable.description: True if any of the org’s AWS connections is behind the current customer-template version. Visible to all members so non-owners know an owner needs to update.

404.description: Not a member, or no such org.

delete.description: Owner only. Removes all members and invites.

200.description: Members of the organization, including GitHub link summaries.

delete.description: Owners can remove anyone; non-owners can only remove themselves. Refuses removal of the last owner.

patch.description: Owner only. Replaces the member’s granted capabilities. The owner’s permissions are implicit and cannot be set.

400.description: Target is the owner, or an unknown permission was supplied.

post.description: Owner only. Hands ownership to another member; the caller becomes a regular member.

get.summary: List a member’s connection bindings in this org
get.description: Self or owner. Which library connection the member uses per provider here.

200.description: The member’s bindings in this org.

put.summary: Choose which of your connections to use in this org
put.description: Connections are personal: only the user themselves can set this (org owners cannot). Points at one of *your own* library connections for this provider in this org. `{userId}` must be the caller.

400.description: Connection does not belong to you, or provider mismatch.

403.description: Only the user can change their own connections.

delete.summary: Clear your connection binding for a provider in this org
delete.description: Connections are personal: only the user themselves can clear this. `{userId}` must be the caller.

403.description: Only the user can change their own connections.

post.description: Sends an invite email and returns the invite URL.

201.description: Invite created. `emailSent` indicates whether SES accepted the message.

get.description: Lets a client render the invite landing page before the recipient signs in.

post.description: Caller must be signed in as the invited email address.

403.description: Invite email does not match the caller.

post.description: An environment template defines the shape of an EC2-based dev environment: services to install, runtimes needed, projects to clone + bootstrap. Owner-only. A full body is validated and starts a bake (`bundle_building` → `ready` / `bundle_failed`). A draft body (`{ draft: true, name, draftState }`) is stored verbatim in `draft` status with no bake, to be resumed and published later.

get.summary: List environment templates in an organisation

200.description: Array of templates with a per-template service/project roll-up (no full nested services/projects — fetch a single template for those).

get.summary: Get an environment template with services + projects

patch.description: Replaces every nested service and project atomically. Send the full desired state, not a diff. If the runtime bundle hash changes, the next launch picks up a re-bake. A draft body (`{ draft: true, name, draftState }`) re-saves the in-progress state and is only allowed while the template is still a draft; sending a full body publishes the draft.

409.description: Draft save attempted on an already-published template

post.summary: Force a fresh AMI bake for an environment template
post.description: Use when a previous bake failed (e.g. transient AWS issue, fixed permissions in the customer account) and you want to retry without first making a definition change. Idempotent on a healthy bundle -- a `ready` bundle stays ready; a `building` one is left alone; a `failed` or absent bundle gets a fresh bake.

200.description: Bake re-triggered; template likely now bundle_building

post.summary: Launch an environment instance ("box") from a template
post.description: Launches an EC2 box in the org's connected AWS account from the template's baked AMI. Requires org membership and an active AWS connection whose runtime bundle is `ready`. Returns immediately with a `pending` box; provisioning runs asynchronously — poll the box until it reaches `running`.

409.description: No active AWS connection, or the template is not baked yet

get.summary: List environment instances in an organisation
get.description: Returns every box in the org (each box’s `mine` flag marks the ones the caller launched); status is refreshed against live EC2 state on read.
Status is refreshed against live EC2 state on read.

delete.description: Restricted to the user who launched the box, or a member with the environments:delete permission (which owners hold implicitly). Terminates the EC2 instance; the box row stays (`terminating` → `terminated`) but is auto-deleted ~30 days later via DynamoDB TTL.

403.description: Not the box launcher or an environments:delete holder

get.summary: Get an environment instance’s host-metrics time series
get.description: Returns the box’s host metrics (CPU, memory, disk, load, network) reported by the daemon, for charting. Any org member may read. Use `?range=` to select the window — one of `30m`, `1h`, `6h`, `24h`, `48h` (default `30m`). Samples are server-side downsampled to at most 240 time-bucketed average points; the `latest` field carries the most recent raw snapshot. Samples are retained for 48h.

schema.description: Time window to return. Defaults to `30m`; unknown values fall back to the default.

200.description: Downsampled metrics time series plus the latest snapshot

post.description: Restricted to the user who launched the box, or a member with the environments:delete permission (which owners hold implicitly).

403.description: Not the box launcher or an environments:delete holder

409.description: Box is terminated or still launching

post.description: Restricted to the user who launched the box, or a member with the environments:delete permission (which owners hold implicitly).

403.description: Not the box launcher or an environments:delete holder

409.description: Box is terminated or still launching

get.description: Any member. Returns the org-level idle-auto-stop + scheduled-sleep policy (or null if unset) and the built-in defaults unset leaves fall back to. Templates can override it per-leaf.

200.description: The org policy (or null) plus defaults.

put.description: Owner only. Idle timeout changes apply to newly launched boxes (the daemon enforces the value baked in at launch); schedule changes apply to running boxes on the next sweep.

put.summary: Set or clear a template’s auto-sleep override
put.description: Owner only. Send `{ autoSleep: <partial policy> }` to override the org policy per-leaf for boxes from this template, or `{ autoSleep: null }` to clear it. Does not trigger a rebake.

get.description: Requires the audit:read permission. Returns org-wide events newest-first. Narrow with `?userId=` (one member’s actions in this org) or `?resourceType=&resourceId=` (one resource). Filter with `?action=`, `?from=`/`?to=` (ISO-8601), `?limit=` (max 100), and page with `?cursor=`.

schema.description: Only events with this action.
Only events at or after this ISO-8601 timestamp.
Only events at or before this ISO-8601 timestamp.
Page size. Values above 100 are clamped to 100.
Opaque pagination cursor from the previous page’s `cursor` field.
Narrow to one member’s actions in this org.
With `resourceId`, narrow to one resource. The resource filter takes precedence over `userId`; ignored without `resourceId`.
With `resourceType`, narrow to one resource.

get.summary: Read your own account audit log
get.description: Your account-level events (login, MFA, API-key lifecycle). Supports the same `?action=`/`?from=`/`?to=`/`?limit=`/`?cursor=` filters.

schema.description: Only events with this action.
Only events at or after this ISO-8601 timestamp.
Only events at or before this ISO-8601 timestamp.
Page size. Values above 100 are clamped to 100.
Opaque pagination cursor from the previous page’s `cursor` field.

get.summary: Read one of your API keys’ activity
get.description: Everything done with this API key, across every org. Owner only. Supports the same `?action=`/`?from=`/`?to=`/`?limit=`/`?cursor=` filters.

schema.description: Only events with this action.
Only events at or after this ISO-8601 timestamp.
Only events at or before this ISO-8601 timestamp.
Page size. Values above 100 are clamped to 100.
Opaque pagination cursor from the previous page’s `cursor` field.

post.description: Mints an external ID and returns a one-click CloudFormation quick-launch URL the customer can run in their own AWS account.

201.description: Pending connection plus quick-launch setup details.

stackStatus.description: Live CloudFormation stack status (e.g. UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS, UPDATE_COMPLETE) when the v9+ template grants DescribeStacks; otherwise absent.

post.description: Slothbox attempts `sts:AssumeRole` against the supplied role ARN with the connection’s external ID. On success the connection moves to `active`.

get.description: Public YAML template customers deploy into their AWS account to grant Slothbox the assume-role permissions it needs.

put.summary: Configure where the org stores its config
put.description: Owner only. Picks the secrets backend (SSM Parameter Store or Secrets Manager), the AWS connection whose account holds the values, and the region. Must be set before any secret or variable can be created — both kinds live in the org's own AWS account, never in Slothbox.

get.summary: List the repos referenced across the org’s templates
get.description: Any member. Repos are deduped by normalized git URL; use `repoId` as the `scope=repo` reference when setting per-repo config.

200.description: Repos used by the org’s templates.

get.summary: List secrets + variables for an org
get.description: Any member. Variable values are fetched from the org account and included; secrets return metadata only (their values are never exposed). Pass `?templateId=` to limit template-scoped entries to one template.

schema.description: Narrow the listing to org-scoped entries plus this template’s entries (repo/environment-scoped entries are excluded).

200.description: Config entries plus the org’s secrets backend settings (or null).

put.summary: Create or update a secret or variable
put.description: Owner only. `{key}` is the env var name. Both kinds are written to the org's connected AWS account (secrets via the chosen backend; variables as SSM String params) and only metadata is kept in Slothbox. Scope determines the refs required: org needs none; repo needs `repoId`; template needs `templateId`; environment needs `environmentId`.

400.description: Validation failed, or no secrets backend configured for a secret.

delete.description: Owner only. Identify the entry with `?scope=` and `?kind=` (plus `repoId`/`templateId`/`environmentId` for non-org scopes). The value is also removed from the connected AWS account. Idempotent.

schema.description: Scope of the entry to delete.
Whether the entry is a secret or a plain variable.
Required when `scope=repo`.
Required when `scope=template`.
Required when `scope=environment`.

get.summary: List runtime bundles in an organisation
get.description: Owner-only. Returns every bundle ever built into any of the org's connected AWS accounts.

get.summary: List bake events for a bundle
get.description: Owner-only. Returns the bake event log in chronological order.
Owner only. Trial state, the card on file (if any), seat count and the resulting per-period total. Every member is a billable seat (£5/seat/mo or £36/seat/yr); new orgs get a 30-day no-card trial.

get.summary: List the catalogue of webhook event types

post.description: Registers an https endpoint to receive signed event deliveries. The signing secret is returned ONCE in the response.

201.description: Created endpoint plus the signing secret (shown once).

patch.summary: Update a webhook endpoint (url, events, or enable/disable)

post.description: Generates a new secret. By default the previous secret keeps verifying for a 24h overlap; pass expireOld=true to cut over immediately.

200.description: The new secret (shown once) and the overlap window.

get.summary: List delivery attempts for an endpoint (newest first)

schema.description: Page size, 1–100 (default 50). Out-of-range values are clamped.
Opaque pagination cursor from the previous page’s `cursor` field.

200.description: A page of deliveries plus an opaque cursor.

post.summary: Replay a delivery (re-send the original event as a new delivery)
